pi_items.source: farmOS Field Kit app documentation - standard log type for harvest operations
Calendar verification - confirmed as valid date, Sunday in 2024
Romney sheep fleece weight analysis: 98 lbs for 12 ewes = 8.17 lbs/ewe, within Romney range of 8-12 lbs per ewe
Dictionary.com, webs sources confirm 'greasy wool' is correct term for raw, unwashed wool with lanolin
Wikipedia and Oklahoma State University - Romney is a real sheep breed known for heavy wool production
farmOS Field Kit app - standard log type for harvest operations
ISO 8601 date format verification - corresponds to May 12, 2024
Romney sheep breed standard - mature ewes produce 8-12 lbs fleece per year; 98 lbs for 12 ewes is realistic
Standard weight unit for wool in English-speaking countries
Wool industry terminology - 'greasy' or 'in-the-grease' is the standard term for unwashed wool
Wool processing terminology - 'skirting' is the standard process of removing vegetable matter and inferior fibers from fleece

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a wool harvest log with authentic agricultural data. Romney is a real sheep breed producing 8-12 lbs of fleece per ewe annually; 98 lbs for 12 ewes (≈8.17 lbs/ewe) is realistic. May 12, 2024 is a valid date. 'Greasy Wool' is the correct industry term for raw, unwashed wool containing lanolin. Skirting refers to the standard wool processing step of removing vegetable matter and inferior fibers. All terminology and quantities are consistent with real-world wool harvesting and sheep breeding practices.
